# UC-17.1.39 · EAP Authentication Method Drift and Weak-Method Regression

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Devices have many ways to prove who they are when joining the network — some are modern and strong, others are decades old and weak. We list which methods are being used and ring the bell when a weak old method is seen, so we can replace it with a stronger one.*

---

## Description

Detects use of weak or deprecated EAP authentication methods (LEAP, EAP-MD5, PEAP+MSCHAPv2 in environments where TEAP or EAP-TLS is the policy standard). Catches policy drift where deprecated methods are silently re-enabled by a misconfiguration or where new endpoint types are negotiating weaker methods than expected.

## Value

EAP-MD5 and LEAP are decades-old methods with known cryptographic weaknesses and should not be in active use. PEAP+MSCHAPv2 is acceptable for legacy clients but FedRAMP / DoD / FIPS 140-3 environments require TEAP or EAP-TLS-only. This UC is the daily auditable evidence that the deployment is enforcing the chosen EAP method policy.

## Implementation

Schedule daily at 06:00 UTC, search window `-1d@d to @d`, trigger on count > 0, no throttle (the daily report is the audit artefact). For real-time regression detection, schedule a second saved search every 30 min comparing the past 30 min's method distribution to a 7d baseline; alert on >2x change in the share of any weak method.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

On a lab endpoint, configure the wireless supplicant to use EAP-MD5. Attempt auth. The auth succeeds at ISE (5400 emitted with EapAuthentication=EAP-MD5). The daily report includes a row with `combined=EAP-MD5` and `is_weak=yes`.

### Negative scenario

Reconfigure the supplicant to TEAP+EAP-TLS. Auth succeeds with `combined=TEAP+EAP-TLS` and `is_weak=strong`. The daily report does not include this method in the weak section.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- ISE *Authentication* category at INFO (default).
- `eap_method_exceptions.csv` lookup defining accepted exceptions per device type.
- `device_inventory.csv` for endpoint context.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5400 earliest=-1h
| stats count by EapAuthentication, EapTunnel
```
For a healthy fleet you should see methods you expect. If MD5 or LEAP appears, that's the symptom this UC catches.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Daily audit search in `spl` field. Schedule daily at 06:00 UTC.

Real-time regression search (every 30 min):
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5400 earliest=-30m
| rex "EapAuthentication=(?<inner>[^,\s]+)"
| stats count by inner
| eval current_share = count / sum(count)
| join inner [
    | inputlookup eap_method_baseline.csv
  ]
| eval delta = current_share - baseline_share
| where abs(delta) > 0.10
```
Where `eap_method_baseline.csv` is built nightly from the 7d distribution.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in controlTest.
Field check: every 5400 event must have `EapAuthentication` non-null. If null, TA version too old.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — EAP Method Distribution":
- Pie: 30d distribution.
- Trend timechart: % share by method last 90d.
- Top-N: users with weak methods.
- Top-N: device types with weak methods (cross-ref `device_inventory.csv`).

Deprecation campaign workflow:
- Daily report → ticket per user/device with weak method → owner assignment → reauth on TEAP/EAP-TLS.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **All methods show as 'medium'** — `is_weak` case has gaps; review the eval.
- **Method counts dropped to zero** — *Authentication* category logging severity raised to WARN; lower to INFO.
- **PEAP+MSCHAPv2 share growing month-on-month** — A new device type is being onboarded with PEAP rather than TEAP. Engage the platform team to update the baseline supplicant config.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=5400
| rex "EapAuthentication=(?<inner>[^,\s]+)"
| rex "EapTunnel=(?<outer>[^,\s]+)"
| eval combined = if(outer=="none", inner, outer."+".inner)
| stats count by combined
| sort - count
| eval is_weak = case(
    like(combined, "%LEAP%"), "yes",
    like(combined, "%MD5%"), "yes",
    like(combined, "%PEAP+EAP-MSCHAPV2%"), "weak_legacy",
    like(combined, "%TEAP%"), "strong",
    like(combined, "%EAP-TLS%"), "strong",
    true(), "medium")
| where is_weak IN ("yes", "weak_legacy")
```

## Visualization

(1) Pie/bar: count of auths by method last 30d. (2) Table: combined method | count | is_weak. (3) Trend timechart: % of auths by method over 90d (stacked). Reveals regression. (4) Top-N table: users still using weak methods (drives the deprecation campaign).

## Known False Positives

- **Legacy device still on-network** — a single old printer doing PEAP+MSCHAPv2 in an EAP-TLS-only environment will appear daily until decommissioned. Maintain `eap_method_exceptions.csv` and exclude.
- **Network test devices** — penetration-test rigs commonly run LEAP; mark and exclude.
- **MAB falls back to no EAP** — MAC-Auth-Bypass sessions don't have EAP; the search filters them via the MessageCode (5400 only emits for EAP successes; MAB uses different codes). Verify your search isn't pulling in MAB.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — EAP Configuration](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
- [RFC 4017 — EAP method requirements (deprecates LEAP)](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc4017/)
- [RFC 9427 — TEAP — Tunnel Extensible Authentication Protocol Version 1](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9427/)
- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (Splunkbase 1915)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1915)
